title: GPX4-Ferroptosis Senolytic Strategy: A New Class of Chloroacetamides Selectively Kills Senescent Cells
description: Imperial College London researchers published in Nature Cell Biology on April 29 that senescent cells are uniquely dependent on GPX4 to survive ferroptosis, a finding exploited to develop a new class of senolytic chloroacetamides from a >10,000-compound screen. The compounds selectively eliminated senescent cells across multiple disease models while sparing healthy cells, and when combined with existing anticancer agents produced a potent one-two punch against persistent senescent tumor cells in melanoma, prostate, and ovarian cancer models. This mechanistically grounded approach differs from first-generation senolytics by targeting an intrinsic vulnerability rather than anti-apoptotic pathways, broadening the senolytic toolkit considerably.

title: RENEWAL-NAD+ Phase 0/1b RCT: Oral LNAD+ Achieves 53% Intracellular NAD Increase Without Raising Plasma NAD
description: The updated preprint (v3, posted April 27) of the RENEWAL-NAD+ randomized controlled trial demonstrates that five days of oral LNAD+, a physicochemically modified NAD+ formulation, elevated intracellular NAD by 53% versus placebo in healthy adults aged 45-75 (p=5.48e-14, Hedges g=3.66) while leaving circulating plasma NAD unchanged. This compartment-selective delivery mechanism is significant because plasma CD38 enzymes normally degrade NAD before cellular uptake, and the trial provides the first human RCT evidence that this bottleneck can be pharmacologically bypassed orally. The finding has direct implications for NAD-based longevity interventions and could reshape formulation strategies across the NR/NMN therapeutic space.

title: Stage-Specific Senescence Transcriptomics Identifies Early Inflammatory Trigger and Mid-Stage Intervention Window
description: A University of Tokyo study published April 29 in Aging-US mapped stage-dependent transcriptomic changes across young, middle, and old human dermal fibroblasts undergoing replicative senescence, revealing that immune and inflammatory gene programs activate during the transitional 'middle' stage well before full senescence is reached. Cell adhesion and maintenance pathways show a concurrent progressive decline, suggesting aging is not a binary switch but a graduated molecular shift with a therapeutically accessible intermediate state. Identifying this mid-stage window as plastic and potentially reversible reshapes thinking about optimal timing for senolytic or senomorphic interventions.

title: FMT from Young Donors Prevents Liver Cancer and Reverses Molecular Aging via MDM2 Suppression in Mice
description: Presented at Digestive Disease Week 2026, University of Texas Medical Branch researchers showed that autologous fecal microbiota transplantation from youthful donor timepoints completely prevented liver cancer in aged mice (0/8 vs 2/8 controls) and suppressed MDM2, an oncogenic liver protein, to youthful levels. The study also documented reversal of inflammation, fibrosis, mitochondrial decline, telomere attrition, and DNA damage, positioning the aging microbiome as an active causal driver of liver pathology rather than a passive marker. The autologous design circumvents immune rejection concerns and strengthens translational potential for human trials, representing a convergence of microbiome science, oncology, and geroscience.

title: XellSmart Phase II Cell Therapy Trial for Parkinson's and Hydra-Rotifer Aging Hypothesis Published in Aging-US
description: Two converging developments in regenerative aging science landed this week: Shanghai-based XellSmart launched a multicenter Phase II trial of its dopaminergic stem cell therapy XS411 in 30 Parkinson's patients aged 50-75, building on Phase I data showing improved motor function, extended on-time, and brain imaging evidence of dopamine recovery without cell-related adverse events. Simultaneously, Aging-US published a hypothesis paper from Geisinger College proposing that introducing Hydra-like FoxO-driven gene expression patterns into rotifers and eventually mammals could delay organismal senescence by enhancing cellular maintenance and proteostasis without full stem cell renewal, offering a fresh theoretical framework for somatic anti-aging strategies.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets GPX4 to Exploit Ferroptosis Vulnerability in Senescent Cells
description: Researchers at Imperial College London's Gil and Tate groups screened over 10,000 electrophilic compounds and identified a new class of senolytic chloroacetamides that kill senescent cells by inhibiting GPX4, an enzyme senescent cells rely on to survive ferroptosis. The approach was validated across multiple disease models and in combination with anticancer therapy in melanoma, prostate, and ovarian cancer models.

title: Stage-Dependent Transcriptomic Changes in Human Dermal Fibroblast Senescence Model
description: University of Tokyo scientists mapped gene expression across three stages of replicative senescence in dermal fibroblasts, finding that immune-inflammatory programs activate early in a 'middle' transitional stage before full senescence, while cell maintenance and adhesion pathways decline progressively. The middle stage emerged as a potentially plastic intervention window for anti-senescence therapy.

title: RENEWAL-NAD+ Phase 0/1b RCT: Oral LNAD+ Elevates Intracellular NAD 53% Without Raising Plasma Levels
description: The latest version (v3) of the RENEWAL-NAD+ preprint reports that five days of oral LathMized NAD+ (LNAD+) elevated intracellular NAD 53% above placebo in healthy adults aged 45-75 (p=5.48e-14) while leaving circulating plasma NAD unchanged, demonstrating compartment-selective delivery. The favorable safety profile and strong pharmacodynamic signal support further development of oral NAD+ repletion for aging applications.

title: From Hydra to Rotifer: New Hypothesis Proposes FoxO-Driven Gene Expression Transfer to Delay Organismal Senescence
description: Geisinger College researcher Michael Bordonaro published a hypothesis in Aging-US proposing that introducing Hydra-like FoxO-driven gene expression patterns into aging-prone rotifers could extend healthspan by improving cellular maintenance and proteostasis without requiring stem cell renewal. The paper outlines an iterative experimental framework from rotifers through Daphnia to mouse validation as a pathway toward somatic anti-aging strategies in humans.

title: NYT Magazine: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: The New York Times Magazine published a landmark feature on cellular rejuvenation science, detailing how researchers have reversed aging in laboratory settings including rejuvenating nonagenarian skin cells, restoring color to graying mice, and reimplanting rejuvenated rat kidneys. The piece contextualizes the first human epigenetic reprogramming trial by Life Biosciences and the broader commercial longevity ecosystem now attracting billions in Silicon Valley investment.

title: Mesoblast Completes Phase 3 Recruitment for Rexlemestrocel-L in Chronic Low Back Pain
description: Mesoblast announced completion of patient recruitment for its pivotal Phase 3 clinical trial of rexlemestrocel-L, its mesenchymal stromal cell therapy, in chronic low back pain, a condition strongly associated with aging. This milestone sets the stage for top-line readout that could support a regulatory filing for the company's cell therapy platform.

title: XellSmart Launches Multicenter Phase II Trial of Dopaminergic Stem Cell Therapy XS411 for Parkinson's Disease
description: Shanghai biotech XellSmart initiated a multicenter Phase II trial enrolling 30 Parkinson's patients aged 50-75 to receive its dopaminergic stem cell therapy XS411 versus standard care over one year, following Phase I data from Beijing Tiantan Hospital demonstrating improved motor function, longer symptom-free periods, and dopamine activity on brain imaging. No cell-related adverse events were observed in Phase I.

title: BlueRock Therapeutics' Bemdaneprocel Shows Sustained Parkinson's Motor Benefits at Three Years in Phase 1
description: Three-year data from BlueRock Therapeutics' exPDite Phase 1 trial, presented at the AAN 2026 Annual Meeting, showed that a single dose of the ESC-derived cell therapy bemdaneprocel maintained stable or improved motor outcomes at three years, with higher-dose patients showing reduced daily off-time and increased on-time without troublesome dyskinesia. The strong safety profile and sustained dopamine function support advancement into the Phase 2 exPDite-2 randomized sham-controlled trial.

title: FMT from Young Donors Eliminates Liver Cancer Risk and Reverses Molecular Aging Markers in Aged Mice
description: University of Texas Medical Branch researchers presented at DDW 2026 that autologous FMT from youthful timepoints prevented liver cancer in all treated aged mice compared to 25% tumor incidence in controls, while suppressing the oncogenic MDM2 protein to youthful levels and reversing inflammation, fibrosis, mitochondrial decline, telomere attrition, and DNA damage. The study repositions the aging microbiome as a causal driver of liver carcinogenesis and aging rather than a passive correlate.

title: Epigenetic Age Acceleration (AgeAccelGrim2) Predicts Alzheimer's Neurodegeneration Pattern on MRI But Not General Brain Aging
description: A study of 1,196 older women published in Aging-US found that the AgeAccelGrim2 epigenetic clock predicts MRI-based Alzheimer's Disease Pattern Similarity Scores but not general brain aging (SPARE-BA), with the association driven primarily by a DNA methylation marker of smoking pack-years linked to frontal and temporal lobe volumes. The findings indicate that different epigenetic clocks capture distinct biological aging pathways and that Grim Age's AD-predictive power is partially mediated by accumulated smoking exposure.

title: Life Time Rolls Out SpiroFit VO2 Max and Metabolic Testing Nationwide Across North America
description: Life Time fitness clubs completed a nationwide rollout of SpiroFit, a cordless wearable metabolic testing system delivering lab-grade VO2 max and five-zone training profiles validated to within 1-3% accuracy. The move democratizes a key longevity biomarker previously accessible only in clinical settings, enabling large-scale data collection on cardiorespiratory fitness trajectories in a non-research population.

title: Mesoblast Acquires Exclusive Global License to Mayo Clinic CAR Technology for Precision-Enhanced MSC Products
description: Mesoblast acquired exclusive worldwide rights to a chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) technology platform developed at Mayo Clinic, with plans to engineer CAR constructs into its FDA-approved MSC products to improve targeted tissue homing and immunomodulatory potency in inflammatory and autoimmune conditions. The platform's initial focus includes CD19-targeting for lupus nephritis and B-cell autoimmune diseases, with Mayo Clinic providing ongoing GMP manufacturing support.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment 2026: Q1 Reaches $3.74B, Projecting $8-9B Full Year
description: Longevity.Technology's Deep Longevity Tracker recorded 49 financing events in Q1 2026 totaling approximately $3.74 billion, a 56% increase over Q1 2025's $2.40 billion, with median deal size of $21.8 million indicating a maturing mid-stage pipeline. Full-year projections based on Q1 momentum and seasonal patterns converge on an $8-9 billion range, implying 55-60% annual growth over 2025's $5.72 billion base.

title: Colchicine Alleviates Non-Atherosclerotic Vascular Aging by Preserving Endothelial Function and Elastin Architecture
description: Erasmus MC researchers showed that chronic low-dose colchicine (0.1 mg/kg/day) significantly reduced arterial stiffness, preserved endothelium-dependent vasorelaxation via NO signaling restoration, and maintained elastin architecture in a smooth muscle-specific ERCC1 knockout mouse model of DNA-damage-driven vascular aging. The findings support colchicine repurposing for primary prevention of vascular aging beyond its established anti-inflammatory role.

title: Longeveron Phase 2b: Laromestrocel Stem Cell Therapy Improves Frailty in Older Adults, Published in Cell Stem Cell
description: Longeveron published Phase 2b results in Cell Stem Cell showing that intravenous laromestrocel (LOMECEL-B), a mesenchymal stem cell therapy, improved the six-minute walk test by 63.4 meters versus placebo at nine months (p=0.0077) in patients with age-related clinical frailty. Nearly one-third of treated patients transitioned from frail to non-frail status, representing the strongest Phase 2b human evidence to date for a cell therapy addressing aging frailty as a primary indication.

title: Klothea Bio Launches Phase 1b Trial of Alpha-Klotho mRNA Therapeutic AKL003 for Lifespan Extension
description: Klothea Bio initiated a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled Phase 1b trial of AKL003, an mRNA formulation in lipid nanoparticles designed to upregulate circulating alpha-Klotho protein, enrolling 21 healthy adults aged 25-75 at GARM Clinic in Honduras. In addition to safety and tolerability, the trial measures exploratory longevity biomarkers including inflammatory markers, metabolic and cardiovascular measures, sleep quality from wearables, and quality of life, making it one of the first clinical trials with explicit longevity endpoints for a Klotho-targeting therapeutic.

title: Lifespan Heritability Revised Upward to ~50% in Science Study Using Twin Databases
description: A Weizmann Institute study published in Science used three twin databases including twins raised apart to show that genetic factors account for approximately 50% of human lifespan variation when deaths from extrinsic causes such as accidents and infections are statistically removed, roughly doubling prior estimates of 20-25%. The revised heritability figure significantly strengthens the rationale for genetic and genomic approaches to longevity intervention.

title: Swiss SWISS100 Centenarian Study Identifies 37 Proteins With Youthful Profiles and Low Oxidative Stress in 100-Year-Olds
description: Researchers from UNIGE and UNIL measured 724 serum proteins in 39 centenarians, 59 octogenarians, and 40 adults aged 30-60, finding 37 proteins in centenarians with profiles resembling those of the youngest group rather than octogenarians, particularly antioxidant and inflammatory markers. DPP-4 preservation in centenarians, which maintains low insulin levels by degrading GLP-1, emerged as a novel metabolic protection mechanism linking centenarian biology to the GLP-1 pharmacology space.

title: GLP-1 Microdosing for Longevity Gains Scientific and Commercial Momentum as AgelessRx Launches RCT
description: AgelessRx initiated a clinical trial examining how sub-therapeutic microdoses of GLP-1 receptor agonists affect healthspan metrics and quality of life, as longevity clinicians increasingly use off-label microdosing for anti-inflammatory and metabolic aging benefits. While rigorous efficacy data remain limited, the trial represents the first controlled effort to evaluate GLP-1 as a formal longevity protocol rather than a metabolic disease treatment.

title: Avaí Bio and Austrianova to Present Alpha-Klotho Cell Therapy Data at Second Annual Klotho Conference
description: Avaí Bio (OTCQB: AVAI) and Austrianova announced plans to present new data from their Klothonova joint venture's alpha-Klotho cell encapsulation therapy at the Second Annual Klotho Conference in September 2026, following production of a GMP Master Cell Bank in March. The encapsulated living cell approach aims to create a sustained internal source of the longevity-associated Klotho protein, addressing a key challenge of its otherwise short circulating half-life.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress: Researchers Reframe Aging as Loss of Biological Coordination Rather Than Damage Accumulation
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity held in Berlin brought together researchers on mitochondria, microbiota, redox biology, and systems medicine to argue that aging is better understood as progressive loss of coordination between biological systems rather than isolated damage accumulation. The framing shift has implications for intervention design, pointing toward multi-system resilience platforms over single-pathway targeting.

title: UT Health San Antonio Prepares Landmark Rapamycin-Semaglutide-Dapagliflozin Three-Drug Longevity Trial with 700 Participants
description: UT Health San Antonio's Barshop Institute announced preparation of one of the largest human trials testing whether rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin in combination can slow biological aging processes in over 700 older South Texas adults. The trial reflects a combinatorial strategy to address multiple aging hallmarks simultaneously and is described by its lead investigator as the culmination of 50 years of aging research at the institution.

title: Longevity Startup Funding 2025-2026: Loyal Raises $100M Series C, Blueprint $60M Growth Round Among Late-Stage Deals
description: Updated longevity funding tracker data shows a maturing funding landscape with Series B and late-stage rounds dominating 2025-2026 activity: Loyal raised $100M Series C for canine longevity drugs, Blueprint secured $60M growth equity, and Function Health raised $298M Series B, alongside continued growth in biological age diagnostics and epigenetic reprogramming platforms. The shift toward later-stage rounds reflects a graduating pipeline of companies beyond early seed and Series A.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences RLS-1496 GPX4-Targeting Topical Senolytic Advances Phase 1 in Psoriasis with Skin Epigenetic Clock Endpoint
description: Rubedo Life Sciences is running a Phase 1 trial of RLS-1496, a topical GPX4 modulator designed to push senescent skin cells toward ferroptosis, in psoriasis with an FDA-cleared IND also covering actinic keratosis. Unusually, the trial measures skin biological age using an epigenetic clock approach alongside disease endpoints, generating proof-of-concept data for whether a senolytic mechanism can modify underlying tissue aging state.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Outlook 2026: Deal Volume Up 80% Year-on-Year in Early 2026, Median Round $7M
description: A secondary funding tracker reports 2026 longevity deal volume is running 80% ahead of the same period in 2025 by deal count, though median round size has fallen from $76M to $7M after stripping out the Retro Biosciences outlier, indicating broad-based early-stage activity rather than a few mega-rounds. The data suggests longevity is attracting a wider field of entrants even as late-stage capital concentrates in a handful of established platforms.
